Normally $170 at full price, this particular set has seen a bunch of price cuts to $150 over 2026, with some occasional drops lower to this same rate. While the deal (or stock) lasts, you’re getting a $34 markdown to add it to your collection at the all-time lowest price we have tracked. Head below to see our roundup of all the other notable LEGO deals leading up to Monday’s holiday.

items.summary:
Amazon is offering the popular Ninja FrostVault Go Backpack Cooler in multiple colorways starting from $149.49 shipped. These handy backpacks are dropping back down from a $200 full price, with most of the deals we’ve seen taking things to the $150 low on the other colorways, while the charcoal grey model is returning to its $149 low. This is a $51 markdown for this one colorway, while the others are getting $50 price cuts – all of them landing at the lowest prices we have tracked. We also spotted the similar Ninja FrostVault wheeled coolers starting from $250 right now, down from $499, while a large selection of the brand’s other appliances and devices can be found with deals in the official Amazon storefront here.

items.summary:
As part of its ongoing Memorial Day sale, Amazon is offering Logitech’s G29 Driving Force Racing Wheel and Floor Pedals kit down at $199.99 shipped. This racing setup has recently been selling for around $330 at Amazon, making today’s offer a solid $130 discount off its usual price. While it’s still about $20 above the all-time low, this is nevertheless the best price we’ve tracked in new condition so far this year. The G29 remains one of the more reliable and popular racing wheel setups on the market, and with Forza Horizon 6 now out, this feels like a particularly good time to finally add a proper wheel-and-pedal combo to your gaming setup. Head below for more details.

items.title: Aiper’s Memorial Day Sale drops newest Scuba V3 AI Vision cordless robot pool cleaner to $900 low, more
items.summary:
As part of Aiper’s Memorial Day Sale with up to $400 savings on robot pool cleaners, you can find a returning low price on its latest Scuba V3 AI Vision Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaner back for $899.99 shipped, which matches the price we’re also seeing over at Amazon. Only on the market since late February, this new robot fetches $1,100 at full price directly from Aiper, while Amazon has seen it climb as high as $1,400. We’ve mostly seen discounts take the costs down between $1,000 and $950, though there have been some occasional falls to the $900 low previously, with that price coming back around during this sale event for $200 savings ($500 off Amazon’s full price tag) at the best rate we have tracked. You can shop the brand’s entire Memorial Day Sale lineup on the landing page here, with some deals also available in the official Amazon storefront here.

items.title: Malaysia To Require Social Media Users To Submit Documents For Age Verification
items.summary:

The move is part of the government’s broader effort to strengthen online safety measures, […]
